title: A standardised protocol for extracting environmental DNA (eDNA) from honey samples of diverse origin
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"bvo6k","text":" We have optimised and validated an in-house protocol for extracting
environmental (eDNA) that is applicable to both processed (Commercial honey
samples) and unprocessed (backyard hive honey samples) honey samples. The protocol
comprises a pre-treatment phase and post-treatment phase for eDNA isolation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dnpu","text":"1. Honey needs to be completely homogenized.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ct4i6","text":"2. Vortex well to make sure no clumps of honey are left in the tubes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e4gkp","text":"3. Complete the whole extraction process in a single day.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"875ka","text":"4. The samples can be weighed (12g of the same honey sample in 4 different 50 mL tubes), which can be done the day before extraction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c9csp","text":"5. The TNE extraction buffer should be pre-heated before adding it to the pellet. Do not heat for more than 5 minutes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qeng","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5di7d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qu7m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ava9n","text":"1. Make sure the TNE buffer is made freshly and stored at 4°C. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mtvu","text":"2. Proteinase K has to be kept on ice during the process.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"95onl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ia7v","text":"1. Do not leave the pellet with elution buffer in water bath for more than 5 minutes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fm31k","text":"      Honey samples","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ntrr","text":"      Ultra-pure water","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4mkvr","text":"      50 mL tubes","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjol0","text":"      5 mL tubes","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eutsa","text":"      1.5 mL and 2 mL tubes","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13ahm","text":"      5 mm Glass beads","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"smnd","text":"      TNE Buffer (pH 7.5) ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8s6jl","text":"      5 M guanidine hydrochloride (w/v) ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"20jn9","text":"      Proteinase K solution (20 mg/ml-1)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":37,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cclum","text":"   6M sodium iodide (NaI)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71u7h","text":"   100 mg/mL silica dioxide (SiO2)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":32,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3un7f","text":"   Silica wash buffer","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"20epi","text":"   10 mM Tris-HCl Elution Buffer (pH 8)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: A total of 50 g of honey was carefully divided into four sterile 50 mL centrifugation tubes, with 12.5 g transferred into each tube. 50 g
40 mL of ultrapure water (Milli-Q) is added to each tube and thoroughly vortexed to ensure complete homogenization. 40 mL
Samples are incubated in a water bath at 40°C for 10 minutes. 40 °C
Samples are then centrifuged at 4700 ×g for 35 minutes. 4700 x g, °C, , 35 minutes
Following centrifugation, the supernatant is carefully discarded, and each resulting pellet is resuspended in 5 mL of ultrapure water. 5 mL
The suspensions were pooled into sterile 50 mL centrifugation tubes corresponding to the original honey samples. 50 mL
The pooled samples are then subjected to an additional centrifugation at 4700 ×g for 30 minutes. 4700 x g, °C, , 30 minutes
After centrifugation, the supernatant is carefully discarded, and the pellet is resuspended in 500 µL of ultrapure water. 500 µL
This suspension is then transferred to a 2 mL microcentrifuge tube containing seven 5 mm sterile glass beads.
The microcentrifuge tubes are vortexed for 2 minutes.
The glass beads are removed using sterile, fine-tipped tweezers.
The suspension was then centrifuged at 11,000 × g at 4 °C for 10 minutes. 11000 x g, 4 °C, , 10 minutes
The supernatant is carefully discarded, and the resulting pellet serves as the starting material for the post-treatment phase.

The pellet obtained from the pre-treatment phase is resuspended in 860 µL of pre-heated (60 °C) TNE extraction buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 150 mM NaCl, 2 mM ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, 1% (w/v) sodium dodecyl sulfate, pH 7.5). 860 µL

To the lysate, 100 µL of 5 M guanidine hydrochloride (w/v) and 40 µL of proteinase K solution (20 mg/mL -1 ) were added to facilitate protein denaturation and prevent the enzymatic degradation of DNA. 100 µL 40 µL
The lysis mixture is incubated at 60 °C for 3 hours in a Thermomixer Comfort (Eppendorf AG, Hamburg, Germany) with agitation at 900 RPM. 900 rpm, 60 °C, , 3 hour
Followed by centrifugation for 15 minutes at 17,000×g at 4° C. 17000 x g, 4 °C, , 15 minutes
The supernatant is collected into a 5 mL tube for DNA purification.
The supernatant is mixed with 2 Volumes of 6 M sodium iodide (NaI) and 100 µL of 100 mg/mL silica dioxide (SiO2). 2 V 100 µL
The samples are allowed to mix well by gentle agitation on a rocker for 30 minutes.
This is followed by centrifugation for 10 minutes at 5000 × g at 4° C. 5000 x g, 4 °C, , 10 minutes
The supernatant is carefully discarded.
The pellet is resuspended in 500 µL of silica wash buffer (50% (v/v) ethanol, 10 mM Tris-HCl, 100 mM NaCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8) by vortexing. 500 µL
This suspension is then centrifuged for 1 minute at 4700 × g at 4° C. 4700 x g, 4 °C, , 1 minute
The wash step (step 23 and 24) is repeated two more times.
The supernatant is carefully discarded.
To elute eDNA from the silica matrix, 50 µL of elution buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8) is added and incubated at 70°C for 5 minutes. 50 µL
Following the incubation, the samples are centrifuged for 5 minutes at 16,000 × g . 16000 x g, °C, , 5 minute
The supernatant is carefully pipetted into a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and stored in -20 ° C. -20 °C
The concentration of isolated eDNA is measured using a Nanodrop Eight spectrophotometer.

The isolated eDNA is validated by performing end-point Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) targeting Apis mellifera mtDNA. Genomic DNA from a healthy adult bee is used as a positive control, and nuclease-free water as a no-template control (ntc).

Amplified PCR products are visualized by agarose gel electrophoresis using a 2% (w/v) agarose gel prepared in 1X TBE buffer (0.13M Tris, 45 Mm Boric acid, 2.5 mM EDTA pH 7.6 ) containing 0.2 µg/mL ethidium bromide.
The DNA bands are visualized under a UV illuminator to confirm successful amplification.
creator: Gopika Kottantharayil
